summary: Injected flatmap-stream dependency contains obfuscated payload targeting cryptocurrency wallets. Attempts to steal private keys from Copay Bitcoin wallet via modified build process.
Hijacked package version includes crypto-miner and credential-stealing trojan. Exfiltrates environment variables and system info to remote C2 server.
Maintainer protest sabotage: infinite loop added to corrupt terminal output. Prints LIBERTY LIBERTY LIBERTY then hangs the process indefinitely.
Protest-ware: overwrites files on disk with heart emoji for users with Russian or Belarusian IP addresses. Destructive payload triggered by geolocation check.
No malicious patterns detected. Well-maintained utility library with extensive test coverage. Minor prototype pollution risk in older versions patched in this release.
No malicious code found. Standard HTTP framework with no suspicious network calls, file system abuse, or obfuscated code patterns.
Backdoor payload targeting bitcoin-wallet Copay. Decrypts AES-encrypted malicious code at runtime, intercepts wallet credentials, and sends them to attacker-controlled server.
Compromised version deploys info-stealer on install via preinstall script. Collects hostname, username, home directory, and sends to remote endpoint.
Hijacked publish includes cryptocurrency miner that runs during npm install. Spawns background process consuming CPU resources.
Clean terminal styling library. No network calls, no file system writes, no dynamic code execution. Safe to use.
